headlines.title: Four swing House races in Pennsylvania loom large for both parties — and for 2028, too
headlines.description: Control of the House of Representatives could come down to four pivotal battleground races in Pennsylvania

headlines.title: San Diego mosque suspects’ writings reveal influence of online extremism, experts say
headlines.description: The two teenage suspects in this week’s deadly attack on a San Diego mosque appear to have written a 75-page document replete with neo-Nazi ideology, incel rage and racist meme culture drawn from the darkest corners of the internet

headlines.title: Justice Department charges Raul Castro with murder
headlines.description: A Florida grand jury indicted Raul Castro, the former leader of Cuba’s Communist regime, with murder and other charges for the 1996 shootdown of American humanitarian airplanes. NBC News Gabe Gutierrez reports.

headlines.title: SpaceX confirms plans for an IPO that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
headlines.description: SpaceX confirmed plans to sell shares of its stock to the public for the first time, according to a securities filing Wednesday outlining the company’s fundamentals

headlines.title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa
headlines.description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Sen. Dick Durbin warns DOJ against ‘doling out compensation to rioters’ in new Trump fund
headlines.description: Durbin, the top Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, asked acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to provide more details about Trump’s $1.8B “anti-weaponization” fund.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Zuckerberg warns ‘success isn’t a given’ after laying off 10% of Meta
headlines.description: As Meta cut 10% of its workforce on Wednesday, CEO Mark Zuckerberg addressed the tech giant’s transition in a companywide memo.

headlines.title: Sen. Dick Durbin warns DOJ against ‘doling out compensation to rioters’ in new Trump fund
headlines.description: Durbin, the top Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, asked acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to provide more details about Trump’s $1.8B “anti-weaponization” fund.

headlines.title: Raúl Castro’s indictment marks important new chapter for the U.S. and Cuba: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Andrea Mitchell examines the next chapter in U.S.-Cuba relations after Raúl Castro’s indictment.

headlines.title: Los Angeles-area wildfires left lead in soil, but how much and where remains contentious
headlines.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency said residents should feel assured that most properties cleared by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers don’t have hazardous amounts of lead. At least one outside scientist is skeptical.

headlines.title: Raúl Castro indictment brings back history of Cuba’s downing of U.S. civilian planes
headlines.description: Cuba shot down two Brothers to the Rescue U.S. civilian planes in 1996, killing four Cuban Americans and drawing swift condemnation from the U.S. and the international community.
